articles.title: Tempo Signs HQ Lease in San Diego to Expand Industrial Electrification
articles.narrative: Tempo, a thermochemical energy company, has leased a 35,000-square-foot facility at 6680 Cobra Way in San Diego's Sorrento Mesa neighborhood for its new corporate headquarters. The company plans to use the space for materials development, engineering, prototype assembly, and system testing as it scales its industrial electrification solutions. This move supports Tempo's transition to broad deployments of factory-built, repeatable products for heavy industry, aiming to replace fossil fuels with electrified heat.

data_points.notable_features: Facility will be used for materials development, engineering, prototype assembly, and system testing for industrial electrification.
data_points.project_notes: Tempo is transitioning to commercial scale and finalizing global gigawatt-scale production capabilities.

articles.title: Paul Singer’s Elliott Management Buys Coconut Grove Hotel for $110M: Updated
articles.narrative: Elliott Management, led by Paul Singer, acquired the Mayfair House Hotel & Garden in Miami's Coconut Grove from Brookfield Asset Management for approximately $110 million. The 180-room hotel, originally built in 1982, was previously purchased by Brookfield in 2019 for $40 million. Acore Capital provided a $79 million loan to the buyer for the transaction.

data_points.notable_features: Adjacent to Virginia Street.
data_points.project_notes: Brookfield Asset Management previously acquired the asset for $40 million in 2019. A deed filed in Miami-Dade County listed the sale price at $69.4 million, likely excluding furniture and operating assets.

articles.title: AMAC Holdings Sells Adjacent East Village Buildings for $23.5M
articles.narrative: AMAC Holdings has sold two adjacent multifamily buildings in Manhattan's East Village to AG Paratus for $23.5 million. The properties, located at 111 and 113 East 12th Street, were acquired by AMAC in June 2018 for $20 million. The transaction highlights continued investor interest in well-located urban residential assets.

data_points.address: 111 East 12th Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 111 East 12th Street is a six-story building with 11 apartments and a retail unit; 113 East 12th Street is a five-story building with 4-5 family units.
data_points.project_notes: AMAC Holdings acquired the properties in June 2018 for $20 million.

articles.title: Midwood Pays $41M for West Village Retail Swath at 403 Avenue of the Americas
articles.narrative: Midwood Investment & Development has acquired a collection of retail storefronts in Manhattan's West Village for $41 million in an all-cash transaction. The properties, located at 403 and 405 Avenue of the Americas and 1 Greenwich Avenue, were purchased from Millsmith, an entity with ties to Samco Properties. This acquisition expands Midwood's presence in the West Village, adding to its diverse national portfolio of retail, office, residential, and development assets.

data_points.address: 403 Avenue of the Americas, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located at the corner of Greenwich Avenue, running toward Christopher Street, two blocks northwest of Washington Square Park.
data_points.project_notes: The seller, Millsmith, acquired the commercial plot in 2007.

articles.title: The Wave is Here: What Multifamily Loan Maturities Mean for Owners, Lenders & Brokers in 2026
articles.narrative: This article analyzes the impending wave of multifamily loan maturities in 2026, highlighting the challenges owners face due to elevated interest rates and tightened underwriting standards. Many owners who secured low-interest loans in 2020-2021 are now over-leveraged, with increased operating expenses, slowed rent growth, and declining valuations making refinancing difficult. This situation is expected to drive a surge in transaction activity, including forced sales, as well-capitalized sponsors re-enter the market to acquire assets that are no longer viable for their current owners. The author advises owners to be proactive, realistic, and plan early for refinancing, selling, or bringing in capital partners.

people.name: Ann Atkinson
people.title: Managing Director of Real Estate Capital Markets

articles.market_intelligence: The Mortgage Bankers Association's 2025 Commercial Real Estate Survey of Loan Maturity Volumes indicates that 13% of multifamily mortgages will mature in 2026. Interest rates reached record lows in 2020-2021, but the Federal Reserve has since raised rates in response to inflation, with the benchmark funds rate between 3.5% and 3.75% in late April. Operating expenses have increased, rent growth has slowed, cap rates have widened, and valuations have declined, leading to tighter underwriting and smaller refinance loan amounts.

articles.title: Harbert South Bay Breaks Ground on 162-Unit Seniors Housing Project in Dallas
articles.narrative: Harbert South Bay has commenced construction on The Marianne on Inwood, a 162-unit seniors housing community in Dallas. This 294,000-square-foot facility will provide a continuum of care options, including independent living, assisted living, and memory care. The project is slated for completion in 2027 and will feature a comprehensive suite of amenities.

data_points.notable_features: Offers independent living, assisted living, and memory care residences. Amenities include multiple dining venues, indoor and outdoor pools, a gym, hair salon, media lounge, and a golf simulator. The site is adjacent to the Highland Park area.

articles.title: Creation Completes 137,000 SF Industrial Project in Haltom City, Texas
articles.narrative: Creation has completed Triad 820, a three-building industrial project totaling 137,000 square feet in Haltom City, a northern suburb of Fort Worth. The development, which began construction in early 2025, offers modern features designed for industrial users. NAI Robert Lynn is handling the leasing for the property.

data_points.notable_features: Features 28-foot clear heights, 125-foot truck court depths, electrical vehicle charging stations, and a secured yard.
data_points.project_notes: Construction began in early 2025.

articles.title: Rockspring Nears Completion of 81-Room Hotel, Convenience Store in Marble Falls, Texas
articles.narrative: Rockspring is nearing completion on an 81-room Home2 Suites by Hilton hotel and a Rodeo convenience store and service station in Marble Falls, Texas. These properties are part of the developer's larger Panther Hollow Village mixed-use development, which has also recently seen the opening of a childcare facility and a bank branch.

data_points.project_notes: Located within the Panther Hollow Village mixed-use development.

articles.title: VMG Health Signs 30,203 SF Office Lease in Downtown Dallas
articles.narrative: VMG Health, a healthcare consulting firm, has signed a 30,203-square-foot office lease in the One Victory Park building in downtown Dallas. Cushman & Wakefield represented the landlord, Clarion Partners, in the transaction.

articles.title: NAI Wisinski of West Michigan marks 15 years
articles.narrative: NAI Wisinski of West Michigan is celebrating its 15th anniversary, marking a significant milestone since the 2011 merger of NAI West Michigan and The Wisinski Group. The firm has grown to become the largest independently owned commercial real estate firm in West Michigan, emphasizing collaboration, local market expertise, and strong client relationships. With 25 licensed commercial real estate professionals facilitating approximately 400 transactions annually and a property management team overseeing over 6 million square feet, the company attributes its success to its people and a consistent focus on client service and adaptability.

data_points.project_notes: Formed in 2011 through the merger of NAI West Michigan and The Wisinski Group.

articles.title: Krusinski Construction Company begins construction of 32,000-square-foot addition to food distribution hub in Aurora
articles.narrative: Krusinski Construction Company has commenced construction on a 32,000-square-foot addition to the Loaves & Fishes Community Services Food Distribution Hub in Aurora, Illinois. This expansion will nearly double the facility's size to 62,000 square feet, significantly increasing storage capacity, improving distribution efficiencies, and enhancing collaboration with partner pantries. The project, part of the organization's Nourish Together campaign, aims to address the more than 50% increase in food insecurity in the surrounding counties since 2019, enabling Loaves & Fishes to serve two to three times more individuals weekly.

data_points.property_name: Loaves & Fishes Community Services Food Distribution Hub
data_points.phase_1: civil engineering work on site and getting a portion of the new East parking lot turned over
data_points.notable_features: Expansion includes new and expanded freezer (2,300 SF) and cooler (3,200 SF) space, a larger depressed dock for semi-trucks, and an improved site layout for parking and recipient pick-up.
data_points.project_notes: The construction is being executed in four phases to minimize disruption to ongoing operations. The facility is designed with a Class III commodity classification.

articles.title: Talonvest Capital provides $48 million in financing for six self-storage properties.
articles.narrative: Talonvest Capital has arranged $48 million in non-recourse refinancing for a portfolio of six self-storage properties on behalf of BRB Development. The financing, provided by a life company, features a seven-year term with full-term interest-only payments and a flexible prepayment structure. The properties are geographically diverse, located across Florida, New Jersey, Minnesota, Connecticut, and Illinois, and benefit from strong local market dynamics.

data_points.notable_features: Portfolio includes two retail units.
data_points.project_notes: The financing included an early rate lock and a 12-month open prepayment window, resulting in over $500,000 in interest savings.

articles.title: The Cooper Commercial Investment Group brokers sale of 36,966-square-foot retail center in North Olmsted
articles.narrative: The Cooper Commercial Investment Group has brokered the sale of Bentley Coe Plaza, a 36,966-square-foot multi-tenant retail center in North Olmsted, Ohio. The property, located in a strong retail corridor, sold for $4.25 million at a 7.38% capitalization rate. The buyer was identified through targeted outreach before the property was formally marketed.

data_points.notable_features: Multi-tenant retail center with strong visibility in a highly traveled commercial area.

articles.title: Josh Flagg takes over listing for Diane Keaton’s former “Pinterest” home
articles.narrative: The former Sullivan Canyon home of the late actress Diane Keaton, known for its unique design inspired by Pinterest, has been relisted for $22.9 million, a $4 million price reduction from its previous asking price. The 9,000-square-foot residence, situated on a 28,000-square-foot lot, was originally purchased by Keaton in 2011 for $4.7 million and extensively renovated. The property, which includes a guest house and pool, was first listed in March 2025 for $29 million and has been on and off the market since.

data_points.address: 13215 Riviera Ranch Road, Los Angeles, CA
data_points.notable_features: Designed by Diane Keaton with architect David Takacs and designers Stephen Shadley, Cynthia Carlson, and Toben Windahl, drawing inspiration from Pinterest. Features five bedrooms, eight bathrooms, an open living room, formal dining room, dual primary suites, a guest house, and a heated outdoor pool.
data_points.project_notes: Diane Keaton purchased the home in 2011 for $4.7 million. The property was first listed for $29 million in March 2025 and later for just under $27 million in December 2025.

articles.title: Ken Mattson reverses course, will plead not guilty in Sonoma Ponzi scheme case
articles.narrative: Ken Mattson, a Sonoma-based investor and developer, has reversed his decision to plead guilty to federal charges in an alleged $100 million Ponzi scheme. Mattson, who was arrested in May 2025, is accused of defrauding hundreds of investors by misrepresenting their ownership stakes in properties managed by his companies, LeFever Mattson Property Management and KS Mattson LP. Prosecutors allege that investor funds were diverted for Mattson's personal use, including luxury real estate and vehicles, with returns paid from new investors or other sources rather than property distributions. He will now proceed with a not guilty plea, leading to a jury trial.

articles.title: Blackstone eyeing Canadian REIT acquisition
articles.narrative: Blackstone is reportedly in preliminary, non-exclusive discussions with Canadian REIT H&R for a potential acquisition of either the entire company or certain assets. This follows a previous attempt by a consortium including Blackstone to acquire H&R last year, which ultimately fell through. H&R, which manages over C$8 billion in assets, has been under pressure from an activist hedge fund to consider a sale due to its stock trading at a discount to its real estate value. The REIT has been strategically rebalancing its portfolio, divesting from retail and office properties to focus on multifamily and industrial assets across Canada and the United States.

articles.title: Billions flow across data center deals despite headwinds
articles.narrative: Despite ongoing controversies and development moratoriums, significant capital continues to flow into the data center sector. KKR has launched Helix Digital Infrastructure with over $10 billion in capital commitments, partnering with Nvidia and Kuwait's sovereign wealth funds to create a comprehensive solution for hyperscalers' data center, power, and connectivity needs. Concurrently, NTT Global Data Centers is seeking to raise at least $1 billion to finance its U.S. projects, with Citigroup assisting in the stake sale of a development company. These moves highlight the continued investment appetite for data centers, even as municipalities grapple with their environmental impact and power consumption.

articles.title: Charlotte giving red light to data center projects for 150 days
articles.narrative: The Charlotte City Council has enacted a 150-day moratorium on new data center developments to study their environmental impact and streamline policy. This pause allows city officials to assess concerns such as noise pollution, water supply effects, and increased demand on local power grids, which have become prominent due to the rapid proliferation of data centers. The moratorium will not affect projects that have already received valid building permits or completed development applications, but it is expected to impact proposed projects like American Tower Corporation's 40,000-square-foot data center and the first phase of PowerHouse Charlotte.

data_points.notable_features: Moratorium enacted for 150 days to study environmental impact of data centers.
data_points.project_notes: The moratorium does not apply to already-approved developments with valid building permits and complete applications.

articles.title: Pocket listings face another setback as New York advances crackdown
articles.narrative: New York's state Legislature has passed the Fair and Transparent Real Estate Listings Act, a bill aimed at increasing transparency in property marketing by requiring agents to publicly market properties unless sellers or landlords explicitly opt out. This legislation, which mandates public listing on widely accessible platforms, is a response to evolving practices in private listings and seeks to ensure broader exposure and competition for properties. If signed into law by Governor Kathy Hochul, it could serve as a model for other states, following similar measures adopted in Washington, Wisconsin, and Connecticut, with Illinois and Hawaii also considering such regulations. Proponents argue that greater transparency benefits consumers by potentially leading to higher sale prices and more offers, while also deterring agents from exclusively pushing private listing networks.

companies_people.label: ASSOCIATION
companies_people.firm_name: New York State Association of Realtors

articles.title: Brandon Miller’s East Village project lands first office tenants
articles.narrative: Two firms have signed 10-year leases totaling over 16,000 square feet at 1 St. Mark’s Place, a nine-story boutique office project in New York City's East Village. An investment firm leased 9,500 square feet, while a technology-focused investment firm took 6,800 square feet. These deals represent a significant step forward for the 60,000-square-foot property, which faced challenges including the developer's death and pandemic-era market conditions.

data_points.address: 1 St. Mark’s Place, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Nine-story boutique office project with curved brick and glass facade.
data_points.project_notes: REEC acquired the leasehold for $29 million. The project was refinanced with a $70 million loan in 2022 after falling behind on payments. Construction was completed in summer 2025. A Sephora store opened on the ground floor in May 2026.

articles.title: “Un-f*cking-believable”: Actors hired for landlord “zombie” demonstration say they feel manipulated
“Shock to the luxury real estate market”: Pied-à-terre tax rollout poses challenges
articles.narrative: New York City's new pied-à-terre tax, set to take effect in July, targets second homes valued over $5 million, including co-op and condo units not used as primary residences. While projected to generate up to $1 billion in revenue, the tax faces significant implementation challenges, particularly concerning residency status verification, property valuation for co-ops, and compliance. Experts anticipate potential litigation and disputes due to ambiguities in the proposed rules, which heavily emphasize penalties for non-compliance rather than clear guidance for homeowners and co-op boards. The retroactive application to January 1, 2026, and the short timeframe for homeowners to prove primary residency status are expected to create substantial hurdles for affected property owners.

data_points.notable_features: Surcharge applies to class one residential properties, co-op units, and condos not used as primary residences by owners, family, or tenants.
data_points.project_notes: Tax applies to properties with market values higher than $5 million; proposed rules detail adjudication of appeals, co-op valuation, and notification processes. Penalties for inaccurate documentation include a surcharge equal to half the tax, plus a 300% penalty on the difference for misleading information.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: New York City Department of Finance (DOF)

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Council of New York Cooperatives and Condominiums

people.name: Stuart Saft
people.title: Leader of New York Real Estate Practice Group

articles.title: Housing Notes: Some streets in the Hamptons just hit different
articles.narrative: The Hamptons housing market is experiencing a significant skew towards higher prices, driven by a "cluster phenomenon" where initial high-value sales on prestigious streets like Meadow Lane create a fear of missing out (FOMO) among other property owners. This dynamic, coupled with limited inventory, a robust Wall Street compensation environment, and an increasing number of cash buyers, is pushing the mix of properties sold into the ultra-luxury segment. The market is evolving from a luxury market to a "billionaire market," with demand shifting towards larger, privacy-focused compounds.

articles.title: From “Pompa-no to Pompa-now”: Mapping Pompano’s latest development boom
articles.narrative: Pompano Beach is experiencing a significant development boom, transforming from a quiet town into a luxury destination with numerous high-end residential and hospitality projects. Developers are drawn to the area's oceanfront land availability and cooperative city environment, offering a more accessible price point compared to neighboring South Florida markets. The influx of branded residences and large-scale apartment complexes is attracting buyers from high-tax states and international markets, signaling a new era of growth and investment for the city.
articles.market_intelligence: Pompano Beach is seeing a surge in luxury developments, including branded residences like Ritz-Carlton and Waldorf Astoria. The city's oceanfront land availability and developer-friendly environment are key drivers. Average sales prices for luxury condos are around $1,600 per square foot. The market is attracting buyers from California, New York, and Canada.

articles.title: $17M in escrow funds gone overnight: Palm Beach law firm sues bank over cyber attack
articles.narrative: A prominent Palm Beach real estate law firm, Rabideau Klein, is suing First Horizon Bank after more than $17 million was stolen from its escrow account in a cyber attack. The firm alleges the bank violated state law governing fund transfers, breached its contract, and was negligent, leading to the unrecovered loss of approximately $6.5 million. The lawsuit details how an unauthorized third party gained access to the firm's online banking platform, changed passwords, created new user IDs, and even obtained a new security token by impersonating a firm attorney, despite numerous red flags that the bank allegedly ignored.

articles.title: Rivani sells waterfront Palm Island home to Jose Mas, partners for $25M
articles.narrative: Commercial real estate investor Robert Rivani sold a waterfront mansion on Palm Island in Miami Beach for $25 million. The buyer was a company led by Jose Mas of the Mas family and architect Cesar Molina, who are actively acquiring and redeveloping homes in South Florida. Rivani had purchased the property six months prior for $22.9 million and undertook renovations, selling it for a $2.1 million profit.

data_points.notable_features: Waterfront property with 220 feet of water frontage, located on a gated island known for privacy.
data_points.project_notes: Seller Robert Rivani purchased the home in November for $22.9 million and renovated it before this sale.

articles.market_intelligence: Palm Island is a gated community in Miami Beach that attracts high-net-worth individuals. A record sale of $45 million for a waterfront mansion at 40 Palm Avenue occurred last year.

articles.title: Todd Boehley’s Eldridge in talks to lease at OKO, Cain’s 830 Brickell
articles.narrative: Eldridge Industries, founded by Todd Boehly, is reportedly in negotiations to lease approximately 8,000 square feet at the 830 Brickell office tower in Miami. This potential move would see the investment firm relocate from its current nearby Miami office. The 57-story tower, developed by OKO Group and Cain, was completed in 2024 and has achieved the highest office rents in Miami-Dade County, benefiting from strong demand driven by an influx of out-of-state firms and favorable tax policies in Florida.

data_points.notable_features: First standalone office building completed in Brickell in over a decade, achieving the highest office rents in Miami-Dade County.

articles.market_intelligence: Miami-Dade County office rents have reached a high of $250 per square foot, gross, at 830 Brickell. South Florida is experiencing increased demand for office space due to an influx of out-of-state firms and migration from states with higher taxes, such as California and New York. Florida has no personal income tax, and a ballot question to increase the homestead exemption for property taxes is pending for the November election.

articles.title: Foreclosed Gold Coast Hilton hits market as conversion candidate
articles.narrative: The Hilton Suites at 198 East Delaware Place in Chicago's Gold Coast, a 30-story, 267,800-square-foot hotel, has been put on the market for sale by LNR Partners, representing bondholders who foreclosed on the property three years ago. The previous owner, Procaccianti, surrendered the hotel via a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure related to nearly $70 million in debt. JLL is marketing the property as a prime candidate for residential conversion, citing existing zoning, or a potential rebranding opportunity, as the Hilton contract expires at the end of 2026.

data_points.notable_features: 30-story hotel located in Chicago's Gold Coast luxury shopping district; existing zoning in place for residential redevelopment.
data_points.project_notes: Property was foreclosed on three years ago; previous owner gave up the hotel through a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure tied to almost $70 million in debt. Hilton Hotels contract expires at the end of 2026.

articles.market_intelligence: CoStar data values the building at $48 million. Chicago's housing market is experiencing a construction pipeline slowdown, leading developers to consider converting unused office space to hotels or residences. The Magnificent Mile is seeing a shift in retail, with more experiential retail and lower-dollar apparel stores emerging alongside luxury brands.

articles.title: Unpaid contractors cloud Obama Center’s finish line
articles.narrative: The Obama Presidential Center, nearing its opening, is facing allegations of unpaid invoices from several subcontractors, reportedly totaling millions of dollars. The project's construction cost has escalated significantly from an initial $300 million to $850 million, attributed to pandemic-related delays and a prolonged legal battle. While the general contractor, Lakeside Alliance, states that contractual closeout processes are normal for a project of this scale, some subcontractors are pursuing legal action to resolve payment disputes, with some expressing regret over their involvement.

data_points.address: 6001 S Stony Island Ave, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: 19-acre museum and library.
data_points.project_notes: Original cost estimate was $300 million. Groundbreaking ceremony was held in 2021, at which point the cost was pegged at $830 million.

articles.title: Prominent Chicago developer Richie Stein dies at 87
articles.narrative: Richard "Richie" Stein, a prominent Chicago developer, passed away at 87. He was known for his risk-taking in business and his commitment to employing minority and woman-owned contractors. Stein & Company played a significant role in shaping Chicago's skyline, including the expansion of McCormick Place and the development of the United Center and AT&T Corporate Center. His work on the AT&T building notably championed diversity in contracting, setting a precedent for the city.

articles.title: Private capital giant Apollo selects Austin as second HQ
articles.narrative: Apollo Global Management is reportedly planning to establish its second headquarters in Austin, TX, a move driven by the firm's talent acquisition strategy and anticipated future growth outside of New York. While specific details regarding the location and required office space are not yet finalized, the private capital giant is expected to select from a range of Class A office options in Austin's downtown and suburban submarkets. This potential relocation comes as Austin's corporate relocation momentum has slowed, prompting the city to consider new incentives to attract businesses.

articles.title: Bray family sells century-old Highland Park mansion
articles.narrative: The Bray Family Trust, known for high-profile real estate investments, has sold a historic 1928 mansion at 4225 Beverly Drive in Highland Park, Dallas. The property, which was last asking $12.8 million, was acquired by Pony Huff GP LLC, an entity managed by Briggs Freeman Sotheby's International Realty agent Bradley Huff. The home features extensive renovations by builder Nickey Oates and interior designer Debra Stewart, including high-end appliances and an elevator, and sold in less than a month after being listed.

data_points.notable_features: Features two laundry rooms, a wine cellar, a four-car garage, and is located near Highland Park Village.
data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired by the Bray family in 2021 when it was asking $7.6 million, after being remodeled by builder Nickey Oates and interior designer Debra Stewart.

articles.title: Shorenstein snags 274K sf office campus in Plano from Spear Street Capital
articles.narrative: Shorenstein Properties has acquired The Tennyson, a fully leased, two-building Class A office campus in Plano, from Spear Street Capital. The property, valued at approximately $70.6 million by the Collin Central Appraisal District, recently underwent significant upgrades to its amenities. The campus benefits from its prime location within Legacy Business Park, offering walkability to major retail and dining destinations and proximity to several corporate headquarters.

data_points.notable_features: Class A office campus with recent upgrades to fitness center, lobby, tenant lounge, and conference rooms. Located in Legacy Business Park, walking distance to Legacy West and The Shops at Legacy.
data_points.project_notes: Renovations completed in 2024. Weighted average lease term is more than six years.

articles.market_intelligence: Plano, TX is experiencing a boom in corporate headquarters, with Samsung moving its U.S. corporate headquarters to the city and Las Vegas Sands opening a tech office. The Collin Central Appraisal District valued The Tennyson at approximately $70.6 million.

articles.title: Musk’s SpaceX eyes 11M sf “Gigasat” factory in Bastrop County
articles.narrative: SpaceX, led by Elon Musk, is planning a significant expansion of its solar production facility in Bastrop County, Texas, to 11 million square feet. This "Gigasat" factory will be dedicated to producing satellites for orbiting data centers, with a goal of launching 6,500 satellites by the end of 2027. The expansion includes facilities for solar ingots, wafers, and AI satellite production, aiming for operational capacity by late 2027.

data_points.notable_features: The facility will produce satellites for orbiting data centers, each handling 150 kilowatts of computing power.
data_points.project_notes: The expansion includes a solar ingots and wafers production facility and an AI satellite factory.

articles.title: Hunington plans $33M multifamily project in booming Fulshear
articles.narrative: Hunington Properties is developing a 348-unit multifamily project in Fulshear, a rapidly expanding town in Fort Bend County, west of Houston. The $33 million development, spanning 444,607 square feet across 12 three-story buildings and a clubhouse, will feature amenities such as a pool court, outdoor exercise area, and a dog park. Construction is slated to commence in September 2026 and conclude by March 2027, contributing to the area's growing housing supply amidst significant single-family development.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities will include a pool court with a cabana, an outdoor exercise area, and a dog park. The project is located within Johnson Development Corporation's 1,350-acre Jordan Ranch master-planned community.
data_points.project_notes: Construction is scheduled to begin September 2026. TDLR filings are preliminary and subject to change.

articles.market_intelligence: Fulshear ranked second among the nation's fastest-growing cities with populations of 20,000 or more last year, with its population growing by 21 percent to 64,630 between July 2024 and July 2025. Celina, TX, grew 24.6 percent to a population of 64,427. Six other Texas cities were in the top 15 fastest-growing cities.

articles.title: Investors eye half-acre site in downtown San Jose for 136-unit development
articles.narrative: An investor group led by Anthony Ho has submitted preliminary plans for an eight-story, 136-unit multifamily development at 945 West Julian Street in San Jose, CA. The project, which includes studio, one-bedroom, and two-bedroom apartments, is notable for being advanced by individual investors rather than a traditional development firm. The developers plan to utilize Senate Bill 330 to streamline the approval process, aiming to contribute to San Jose's housing goals.

data_points.address: 945 West Julian Street, San Jose, CA
data_points.notable_features: Located within walking distance of Diridon Station and the SAP Center.
data_points.project_notes: Developers intend to use provisions of Senate Bill 330 to streamline the approval process.

articles.market_intelligence: The City of San Jose must plan for 62,200 new housing units by 2031 as part of its state-mandated housing construction requirements.

articles.title: Apple strikes again in Sunnyvale with a $162M office pickup
articles.narrative: Apple has acquired an office building at 684 West Maude Avenue in Sunnyvale, CA for $162.2 million. The tech giant purchased the property from Union Investment Real Estate GmbH and Metzler Real Estate Advisors, securing a significant discount from its previous sale price in 2022. This acquisition continues Apple's trend of expanding its real estate footprint in the Silicon Valley region, particularly in Sunnyvale and Cupertino.

data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired in 2022 for $222 million. LinkedIn leased the building in 2022, but it is unclear if they ever occupied the space.

articles.title: Hudson Pacific lands SF’s biggest office lease in nearly a decade
articles.narrative: The City and County of San Francisco has signed a significant 502,000-square-foot office lease expansion at 1455 Market Street, owned by Hudson Pacific Properties. This deal brings the city government's total footprint in the building to over 900,000 square feet and is the largest office lease in San Francisco in nearly a decade. The 23-year lease term includes options for two five-year extensions and a right for the city to purchase the building, reflecting a strong commitment to consolidating several city departments into a modern, seismically sound facility. This move is expected to boost occupancy in the building to 89% and free up a city-owned property for future housing and mixed-use development.

data_points.notable_features: 22-story building; city government will consolidate multiple departments into this modern facility.
data_points.project_notes: The City and County of San Francisco already occupies approximately 400,000 square feet in the building from two prior leases signed in 2024 and 2025. The city has the right to purchase the building until March 2027 and a right of first offer thereafter. Hudson Pacific Properties previously acquired the building for $93 million in 2010 and bought out a joint venture partner's interest for $43.5 million in 2024, valuing the property at $96.6 million at that time.

companies_people.label: TENANT
companies_people.firm_name: City and County of San Francisco

people.name: Angela Yip
people.title: spokesperson for the city’s real estate division

articles.title: Native American tribe grows Seattle-area holdings with $53M apartment buy
articles.narrative: The Spokane Tribe of Indians, through its Spokane Indian Housing Authority, has acquired the 200-unit Waterford Apartments in Everett, WA, for $53.3 million. This transaction marks the largest multifamily sale in Everett this year and continues the housing authority's strategy of expanding its real estate holdings in the Seattle metropolitan area. The property, built in 1989 and renovated in 2014, features a strong amenity package. The acquisition aligns with the buyer's recent trend of purchasing multifamily assets in the region, often with plans to convert market-rate units into income-restricted affordable housing.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities include indoor and outdoor pools, a fitness center, a dog park, and a sauna and spa. Units underwent renovations in 2014.
data_points.project_notes: The property last sold in October 2016 for $43.2 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Everett is experiencing strong short- and long-term rent growth due to limited new multifamily deliveries and steady renter demand. Everett's occupancy rate was 94.9% in Q1, compared to Seattle's 94.2%. While Seattle saw 524 new multifamily units completed in Q1, Everett had zero.

articles.title: Clovis 7-Eleven C-Store/Gas Station Trades in 1031 Exchange
articles.narrative: Hanley Investment Group Real Estate Advisors has completed the $5 million sale of a 7-Eleven convenience store and gas station in Clovis, CA. The single-tenant, fee-simple property was acquired by a 1031 exchange investor from Bakersfield. The transaction highlights the strong demand from private investors for properties offering bonus depreciation eligibility, absolute triple-net lease structures, and strong tenant performance, according to the seller's broker.

data_points.notable_features: Absolute triple-net lease structure; grocer anchor.
data_points.project_notes: Acquired by a 1031 exchange investor; eligible for 100% bonus depreciation.

companies_people.label: SELLER BROKER
companies_people.firm_name: Hanley Investment Group Real Estate Advisors

articles.title: Pet Food Manufacturer Signs 163K-SF Lease in Ontario
articles.narrative: Hoa’s Global Pet Nutrition Inc. has signed a 163,336-square-foot industrial lease at 1930 S. Parco Ave. in Ontario, CA. The facility's unique features, including heavy power, a Class 8 Clean Room, and strategic access to freeways and airports, were key factors in securing the deal. This lease supports the tenant's expansion plans and highlights the property's versatility for various industrial operations in the Inland Empire market.

data_points.notable_features: M2-zoned property with heavy power, fully fenced perimeter, fully built-out office space, conference rooms, training room, and a 10,000-square-foot Class 8 Clean Room. Offers flexibility for warehousing, distribution, manufacturing, and logistics.

articles.title: Former Performing Arts School Sells to Summa Academy as Its Third Bay Area Campus
articles.narrative: Summa Academy, a non-public school for students with autism and special needs, acquired a 43,702-square-foot former performing arts school facility at 2730 Mitchell Drive in Walnut Creek, CA, for $7.1 million. This acquisition marks Summa Academy's third and largest campus in the East Bay, providing expanded space for individualized education and life-skills development, including a mock apartment. Brokers involved in the transaction emphasized the importance of preserving the site for educational use within the community.

data_points.notable_features: Former performing arts school, includes a mock apartment with laundry room and kitchen for life-skills development.

companies_people.label: SELLER
companies_people.firm_name: Contra Costa School of Performing Arts

articles.title: Sentinel Real Estate Acquires Luxury Apartment Community in Potomac
articles.narrative: Sentinel Real Estate has acquired The Perry, a luxury multifamily community in Potomac, Maryland. The property, completed in 2016, features 297 residential units and 8,610 square feet of ground-floor retail. This acquisition represents a rare opportunity to invest in a premier residential community within one of the nation's most affluent submarkets.

data_points.notable_features: Luxury multifamily community with modern construction, spacious floor plans, high-end interior finishes, and comprehensive indoor and outdoor amenities.

articles.title: Vornado Closes on 49% Stake in Fisher Brothers’ Park Avenue Plaza
articles.narrative: Vornado Realty Trust has finalized its acquisition of a 49% stake in Park Avenue Plaza, a 1.2-million-square-foot Class A office tower in Midtown Manhattan. The transaction valued the property at $1.1 billion, with Vornado acquiring its interest subject to its share of the existing $575 million loan. Fisher Brothers will retain its 51% ownership and continue to manage and lease the property, with both parties holding joint control over major decisions.

data_points.address: 55 E. 52nd St., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Trophy Class A office building with protected Park Avenue views, occupying a full through-block between East 52nd and East 53rd streets.
data_points.project_notes: The property is encumbered by a $575 million loan with a fixed interest rate of 2.99% maturing in November 2031. Vornado acquired a 49% interest, with Fisher Brothers retaining 51% ownership and management responsibilities.

articles.title: 'Infrastructure Of The Future': $75B SpaceX IPO To Fuel Data Center Expansion On Land And In Orbit
articles.narrative: SpaceX's recent $75 billion initial public offering, which valued the company at $1.7 trillion, is expected to significantly fuel its expansion in the data center sector, both on Earth and in orbit. The company, which has operations across 12 major properties in six states, plans to leverage this capital to further develop its terrestrial data center infrastructure, particularly in Texas, Tennessee, and Mississippi, where it is already building some of the country's largest facilities. Additionally, a substantial portion of the IPO proceeds will support Elon Musk's ambitious plans to launch 1 million solar-powered satellites into low Earth orbit to serve as AI computing data centers, a move he believes will be more energy-efficient than ground-based alternatives.

articles.title: Monty Bennett To Collect $480M Breakup Fee As Luxury Hotel REIT Goes Independent
articles.narrative: Braemar Hotels & Resorts, a luxury hotel REIT, is becoming a self-managed entity and terminating its advisory agreements with Monty Bennett's Ashford Inc. This decision follows a contentious and ultimately failed attempt to sell Braemar, which would have resulted in a significant payout to Bennett. To fund the $480 million termination fee owed to Ashford, Braemar is selling three of its hotels, including the Ritz-Carlton in Sarasota, Florida, for $437.5 million, and plans to divest additional assets. The move aims to simplify Braemar's corporate structure, enhance operational efficiency, and create long-term shareholder value, with new independent board members to be appointed.

data_points.project_notes: Braemar Hotels & Resorts is selling three hotels, including the Ritz-Carlton in Sarasota, FL, for $437.5 million to fund a $480 million termination fee to Ashford Inc.
